notice: No cross-language mapping is asserted by this file — only the lexicon of each side. Authority per source is in metadata.source_authority; some entries (SHACL Compact Syntax, tree-sitter TypeScript) carry a per-entry `authority_status` field marking them as community-draft / community-grammar.

source_authority.shacl_compact_syntax: W3C Community Group draft — NOT a Recommendation. Pinned to w3c/shacl commit e283899 (2025-01-27).
source_authority.typescript_grammar: Community grammar (tree-sitter-typescript v0.23.2) — answers 'what does this parser emit' rather than 'what does the TypeScript compiler recognize'. For an authoritative TypeScript keyword inventory the compiler's SyntaxKind enum (src/compiler/types.ts) is closer to the truth; the two surfaces can disagree.
source_authority.typescript_lib: Reference implementation (Microsoft TypeScript v6.0.3) — no normative EBNF exists for TypeScript; lib.*.d.ts IS the definition of the intrinsic types it declares.
source_authority.zod_v3: Published npm distribution (zod@3.24.4) — library API, no formal spec
source_authority.zod_v4: Published npm distribution (zod@4.4.3) — library API, no formal spec
source_authority.linkml: Community specification (linkml/linkml-model v1.11.0) — NOT a W3C/IETF standard. The metamodel is self-describing (meta.yaml is itself a LinkML schema). Adopted in biomedical data modeling (NIH/CD2H, NCATS, Monarch Initiative). Governance: open-source maintainers under the `linkml` GitHub org.
source_authority.python: Reference implementation (CPython v3.12.13) — Python is governed by PEPs / the Steering Council; CPython is the reference implementation. Lib/keyword.py is auto-generated from the canonical grammar (Grammar/python.gram), so its lists ARE the language's reserved-word surface. The built-in functions/classes/exceptions scraped from Doc/library/*.rst are the documented surface as shipped with this CPython tag; dunders are deliberately omitted (not deterministically enumerable from a single file).
source_authority.protobuf: Reference implementation (protocolbuffers/protobuf v34.1) — Protobuf is governed by Google; the protobuf monorepo is the canonical reference. descriptor.proto is self-describing — it defines the message types that describe all other messages, and contains the FieldDescriptorProto.Type enum which IS the scalar-type inventory. The well-known types are documented at protobuf.dev/reference/protobuf/google.protobuf and shipped as .proto files under src/google/protobuf/. Language keywords are deliberately omitted: no machine-readable grammar artifact exists in the protobuf repo (the EBNF lives in prose form in the spec docs at a separate repo, protocolbuffers.github.io). Built-in functions are n/a — Protobuf is a schema language, not a runtime.
source_authority.postgres: Reference implementation (postgres/postgres REL_18_4) — PostgreSQL is governed by the PostgreSQL Global Development Group; the postgres monorepo is the canonical reference. The kwlist.h PG_KEYWORD macros are consumed by gram.y at build time, so they ARE the language's reserved-word surface (no looser definition exists). The .dat files under src/include/catalog/ are compiled by genbki.pl into the initial template1 database — they ARE the built-in catalog. PG 18 also implements parts of the SQL:2023 standard, but where the implementation diverges from the standard (or extends it: jsonb operators, range types, system catalogs), this lexicon reflects what PG ships, not what SQL:2023 mandates.

extraction_rules.json_schema_keywords: Keys of the top-level 'properties' object in each 2020-12 vocabulary meta-schema; the value is preserved as the keyword's value-constraint schema. The `format` keyword appears twice — once in the format-annotation vocabulary, once in format-assertion — and is disambiguated by the `vocabulary` field. Note that `format` admits a string enumeration of values (date-time, uuid, ipv4, …) which is NOT enumerated here; see JSON Schema 2020-12 Validation §7.3 for the value set. The `comment` field captures any `$comment` string sibling of the keyword's value-constraint schema. As of draft 2020-12 only one such comment exists across all 8 vocabularies (on `$id` in the core vocabulary); the field's low occupancy reflects the source, not extractor loss.
extraction_rules.xsd_elements: Every <xs:element name='...'/> declaration (top-level + nested) in the schema-for-schemas. Each entry carries `parent_path`, an XPath-like ancestry that disambiguates sibling declarations of the same name (e.g. <xs:element name='simpleType'/> appears in multiple parent definitions inside XMLSchema.xsd). Uniqueness key: (source, name, parent_path). (name, parent_path) alone is NOT unique — Part 1 and Part 2 schema-for-schemas reuse the same paths for the same names; consumers must include `source` (or `source_url`) when indexing. The `documentation` field captures the text of any direct <xs:annotation><xs:documentation> child. Only 2 elements carry one in the W3C schema-for-schemas (Part 1 `facet`, Part 2 `facetElement`); low occupancy reflects the source, not extractor loss.
extraction_rules.xsd_attributes: Every <xs:attribute name='...'/> declaration (top-level + nested) in the schema-for-schemas. Each entry carries `parent_path` (same semantics as for xsd_elements). Uniqueness key: (source, name, parent_path). Each entry also carries `use` ∈ {required, optional, prohibited} (default: optional) and `form` ∈ {qualified, unqualified} (default: the schema's attributeFormDefault). Both are recorded as the raw attribute value when present in source, or None when omitted (consumer applies the spec defaults).
extraction_rules.xsd_datatypes: lxml: every <h4> with a child <a id='NAME'> in XSD 1.1 Part 2, filtered to those whose section number is §3.2–3.4.
extraction_rules.xsd_datatype_productions: Numbered EBNF productions [N] name ::= rhs from XSD 1.1 Part 2 HTML, extracted from the lxml-normalised serialisation. Terminator is (Part 2 main) or (Appendix G regex grammar).
extraction_rules.shacl_core_vocabulary: Every block beginning ^sh:Name at column 0 in the Turtle ontology, terminated by the next ^sh: or EOF.
extraction_rules.shacl_compact_syntax_grammar: lxml: locate the <pre> element containing 'grammar SHACLC;', then parse 'name : body ;' rules from its text content.
extraction_rules.json_ld_keywords: lxml: section[@id='syntax-tokens-and-keywords'] in the JSON-LD 1.1 Recommendation; walk the <dl>'s <dt>/<dd> pairs, keeping entries whose <dt> begins with '@'. Scope note: the @-prefix filter excludes non-@ syntactic tokens that the same section also lists (e.g. ':' as the compact-IRI separator) and excludes @version *values* like '1.0'/'1.1' which are not keywords themselves. Consumers needing those should consult §1.7 directly.
extraction_rules.typescript_syntactic_constructs: Named, non-internal node types in tree-sitter-typescript's node-types.json.
extraction_rules.typescript_intrinsic_types_es5: Every ^interface Name and ^type Name declaration in TypeScript's lib.es5.d.ts. Scope is es5 only — modern intrinsic types defined in lib.es2015.d.ts … lib.es2024.d.ts, lib.dom.d.ts, etc. (Promise, Map, Set, WeakRef, …) are NOT covered.
extraction_rules.zod_distribution_exports: Every ^export (declare )?(class|interface|type|function|const|enum) Name in each Zod .d.ts distribution file, plus named re-exports ^export [type] { ... } from. Direct declarations win over re-exports of the same name.
extraction_rules.linkml_metamodel: Three buckets from the self-describing LinkML metamodel: (classes) keys of meta.yaml's `classes:` block; (slots) keys of meta.yaml's `slots:` block — the metaslots writable in any LinkML schema; (types) keys of types.yaml's `types:` block — the 19 built-in scalar types. The root meta.yaml also `imports:` companion modules (annotations, mappings, extensions, units) which define additional slots not captured here; the imports list is preserved in the extracted payload for downstream resolution. Imports records: `source_url` is the declaration site (meta.yaml — where the `imports:` directive appears, NOT the file the directive resolves to). `resolves_to` is the target file in the linkml-model repo (or None when the `linkml:<X>` prefix-convention does not apply).
extraction_rules.cross_language_index: Computed at payload-build time, not scraped. Walks every list-of-records lexicon section and indexes entry names by (section, sub_key). Result: name → sorted list of `<section>.<sub_key>` locations, filtered to names appearing in ≥ 2 *distinct* sub-keys (not just multiple times within one section). Asserts NO semantic equivalence — same-string presence only. Consumers must validate any actual cross-language mapping themselves; the top-level `notice` is the relevant disclaimer. Derived artifact: cross_language_index has NO source_authority entry because it is not an external source — its authority is the SHA-256 provenance of every section it indexes.
extraction_rules.python_lexicon: Five buckets from CPython's source: (keywords, soft_keywords) extracted as string literals from the `kwlist = [...]` and `softkwlist = [...]` module-level assignments in Lib/keyword.py (which is auto-generated from Grammar/python.gram). (builtin_functions, builtin_classes) from `.. function::` and `.. class::` rst directives in Doc/library/functions.rst — the 19 class-directives ARE the built-in type-constructor inventory. (builtin_exceptions) from `.. exception::` directives in Doc/library/exceptions.rst. Dunder methods are deliberately OMITTED: they are not deterministically enumerable from any single source-of-truth file in cpython (only ~46 of ~100+ real dunders are documented as rst directives; the rest live in prose tables across multiple files).
extraction_rules.protobuf_lexicon: Three buckets extracted with line-oriented regex against pinned .proto source: (scalar_types) the 18 TYPE_* values from FieldDescriptorProto.Type in descriptor.proto, tagged `kind: scalar` (15) or `kind: composite` (3 — TYPE_GROUP, TYPE_MESSAGE, TYPE_ENUM, whose actual type is carried in a separate type_name field). (well_known_types) every top-level `message X` or `enum X` declaration from a CLOSED ALLOWLIST of 10 canonical WKT files (any.proto, api.proto, duration.proto, empty.proto, field_mask.proto, source_context.proto, struct.proto, timestamp.proto, type.proto, wrappers.proto), emitted as fully-qualified `google.protobuf.<Name>`. The allowlist is mandatory: src/google/protobuf/ also contains ~62 unittest fixtures, internal helpers, and edition-features files that are NOT part of the WKT surface. (field_options) optional/repeated field names from the FieldOptions message in descriptor.proto. Language keywords are deliberately OMITTED — no machine-readable grammar artifact exists in the protobuf repo (the EBNF lives in prose form in the spec docs at a separate repo, protocolbuffers.github.io).
extraction_rules.postgres_lexicon: Five buckets from pinned PostgreSQL sources. (keywords) every `PG_KEYWORD(name, token, category, bare_label)` macro in src/include/parser/kwlist.h; the four categories — RESERVED_KEYWORD, UNRESERVED_KEYWORD, COL_NAME_KEYWORD, TYPE_FUNC_NAME_KEYWORD — are preserved as a per-entry field. (types) every `typname => 'name'` in pg_type.dat — the 112 built-in types. (functions) every `proname => 'name'` in pg_proc.dat, deduplicated by name; the 615 names that have multiple signatures retain their multiplicity in an `overload_count` field (so the 3397 source entries collapse to 2782 unique names without losing the overload-pressure signal). (operators) every `oprname => 'symbol'` in pg_operator.dat, same dedup-with-overload-count strategy (74 unique symbols from 799 source entries — overload-by-operand-type is implementation detail, not lexicon). (casts) every `(castsource, casttarget)` pair in pg_cast.dat — the 235 built-in cast edges between types. These files compile into the initial template1 database via genbki.pl; they ARE the catalog.

metadata.disclaimer: All entries are scraped from authoritative or authority-equivalent sources. Authority level per lexicon is recorded in source_authority; SHA-256 + byte_length per fetched URL is recorded in source_provenance for reproducibility. No cross-language mapping is asserted by this file — only the lexicon of each side.

elements.name: facet
elements.documentation: An abstract element, representing facets in general. The facets defined by this spec are substitutable for this element, and implementation-defined facets should also name this as a substitution-group head.

elements.name: facet
elements.documentation: An abstract element, representing facets in general. The facets defined by this spec are substitutable for this element, and implementation-defined facets should also name this as a substitution-group head.

datatypes.name: anySimpleType
datatypes.prose_excerpt: The definition of anySimpleType is a special ·restriction· of anyType. The ·lexical space· of anySimpleType is the set of all sequences of Unicode characters, and its ·value space· includes all ·atomic values· and all finite-length lists of zero or more ·atomic values·.

datatypes.name: anyAtomicType
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] anyAtomicType is a special ·restriction· of anySimpleType. The ·value· and ·lexical spaces· of anyAtomicType are the unions of the ·value· and ·lexical spaces· of all the ·primitive· datatypes, and anyAtomicType is their ·base type·.

datatypes.name: string
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] The string datatype represents character strings in XML.

datatypes.name: boolean
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] boolean represents the values of two-valued logic.

datatypes.name: decimal
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] decimal represents a subset of the real numbers, which can be represented by decimal numerals. The ·value space· of decimal is the set of numbers that can be obtained by dividing an integer by a non-negative power of ten, i.e., expressible as i / 10n where i and n are integers and n ≥ 0. Precision is not reflected in this value space; the number 2.0 is not distinct from the number 2.

datatypes.name: float
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] The float datatype is patterned after the IEEE single-precision 32-bit floating point datatype [IEEE 754-2008]. Its value space is a subset of the rational numbers. Floating point numbers are often used to approximate arbitrary real numbers.

datatypes.name: double
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] The double datatype is patterned after the IEEE double-precision 64-bit floating point datatype [IEEE 754-2008]. Each floating point datatype has a value space that is a subset of the rational numbers. Floating point numbers are often used to approximate arbitrary real numbers.

datatypes.name: duration
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] duration is a datatype that represents durations of time. The concept of duration being captured is drawn from those of [ISO 8601], specifically durations without fixed endpoints. For example, "15 days" (whose most common lexical representation in duration is "'P15D'") is a duration value; "15 days beginning 12 July 1995" and "15 days ending 12 July 1995" are not duration values. dur

datatypes.name: dateTime
datatypes.prose_excerpt: dateTime represents instants of time, optionally marked with a particular time zone offset. Values representing the same instant but having different time zone offsets are equal but not identical.

datatypes.name: time
datatypes.prose_excerpt: time represents instants of time that recur at the same point in each calendar day, or that occur in some arbitrary calendar day.

datatypes.name: date
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] date represents top-open intervals of exactly one day in length on the timelines of dateTime, beginning on the beginning moment of each day, up to but not including the beginning moment of the next day). For non-timezoned values, the top-open intervals disjointly cover the non-timezoned timeline, one per day. For timezoned values, the intervals begin at every minute and therefore ove

datatypes.name: gYearMonth
datatypes.prose_excerpt: gYearMonth represents specific whole Gregorian months in specific Gregorian years.

datatypes.name: gYear
datatypes.prose_excerpt: gYear represents Gregorian calendar years.

datatypes.name: gMonthDay
datatypes.prose_excerpt: gMonthDay represents whole calendar days that recur at the same point in each calendar year, or that occur in some arbitrary calendar year. (Obviously, days beyond 28 cannot occur in all Februaries; 29 is nonetheless permitted.)

datatypes.name: gDay
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] gDay represents whole days within an arbitrary month—days that recur at the same point in each (Gregorian) month. This datatype is used to represent a specific day of the month. To indicate, for example, that an employee gets a paycheck on the 15th of each month. (Obviously, days beyond 28 cannot occur in all months; they are nonetheless permitted, up to 31.)

datatypes.name: gMonth
datatypes.prose_excerpt: gMonth represents whole (Gregorian) months within an arbitrary year—months that recur at the same point in each year. It might be used, for example, to say what month annual Thanksgiving celebrations fall in different countries (--11 in the United States, --10 in Canada, and possibly other months in other countries).

datatypes.name: hexBinary
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] hexBinary represents arbitrary hex-encoded binary data.

datatypes.name: base64Binary
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] base64Binary represents arbitrary Base64-encoded binary data. For base64Binary data the entire binary stream is encoded using the Base64 Encoding defined in [RFC 3548], which is derived from the encoding described in [RFC 2045].

datatypes.name: anyURI
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] anyURI represents an Internationalized Resource Identifier Reference (IRI). An anyURI value can be absolute or relative, and may have an optional fragment identifier (i.e., it may be an IRI Reference). This type should be used when the value fulfills the role of an IRI, as defined in [RFC 3987] or its successor(s) in the IETF Standards Track.

datatypes.name: QName
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] QName represents XML qualified names. The ·value space· of QName is the set of tuples {namespace name, local part}, where namespace name is an anyURI and local part is an NCName. The ·lexical space· of QName is the set of strings that ·match· the QName production of [Namespaces in XML].

datatypes.name: NOTATION
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] NOTATION represents the NOTATION attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of NOTATION is the set of QNames of notations declared in the current schema. The ·lexical space· of NOTATION is the set of all names of notations declared in the current schema (in the form of QNames).

datatypes.name: normalizedString
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] normalizedString represents white space normalized strings. The ·value space· of normalizedString is the set of strings that do not contain the carriage return (#xD), line feed (#xA) nor tab (#x9) characters. The ·lexical space· of normalizedString is the set of strings that do not contain the carriage return (#xD), line feed (#xA) nor tab (#x9) characters. The ·base type· of normali

datatypes.name: token
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] token represents tokenized strings. The ·value space· of token is the set of strings that do not contain the carriage return (#xD), line feed (#xA) nor tab (#x9) characters, that have no leading or trailing spaces (#x20) and that have no internal sequences of two or more spaces. The ·lexical space· of token is the set of strings that do not contain the carriage return (#xD), line fee

datatypes.name: language
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] language represents formal natural language identifiers, as defined by [BCP 47] (currently represented by [RFC 4646] and [RFC 4647]) or its successor(s). The ·value space· and ·lexical space· of language are the set of all strings that conform to the pattern [a-zA-Z]{1,8}(-[a-zA-Z0-9]{1,8})* This is the set of strings accepted by the grammar given in [RFC 3066], which is now obsolete

datatypes.name: NMTOKEN
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] NMTOKEN represents the NMTOKEN attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of NMTOKEN is the set of tokens that ·match· the Nmtoken production in [XML]. The ·lexical space· of NMTOKEN is the set of strings that ·match· the Nmtoken production in [XML]. The ·base type· of NMTOKEN is token.

datatypes.name: NMTOKENS
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] NMTOKENS represents the NMTOKENS attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of NMTOKENS is the set of finite, non-zero-length sequences of ·NMTOKEN·s. The ·lexical space· of NMTOKENS is the set of space-separated lists of tokens, of which each token is in the ·lexical space· of NMTOKEN. The ·item type· of NMTOKENS is NMTOKEN. NMTOKENS is derived from ·anySimpleType· in two steps: a

datatypes.name: Name
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] Name represents XML Names. The ·value space· of Name is the set of all strings which ·match· the Name production of [XML]. The ·lexical space· of Name is the set of all strings which ·match· the Name production of [XML]. The ·base type· of Name is token.

datatypes.name: NCName
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] NCName represents XML "non-colonized" Names. The ·value space· of NCName is the set of all strings which ·match· the NCName production of [Namespaces in XML]. The ·lexical space· of NCName is the set of all strings which ·match· the NCName production of [Namespaces in XML]. The ·base type· of NCName is Name.

datatypes.name: ID
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] ID represents the ID attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of ID is the set of all strings that ·match· the NCName production in [Namespaces in XML]. The ·lexical space· of ID is the set of all strings that ·match· the NCName production in [Namespaces in XML]. The ·base type· of ID is NCName.

datatypes.name: IDREF
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] IDREF represents the IDREF attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of IDREF is the set of all strings that ·match· the NCName production in [Namespaces in XML]. The ·lexical space· of IDREF is the set of strings that ·match· the NCName production in [Namespaces in XML]. The ·base type· of IDREF is NCName.

datatypes.name: IDREFS
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] IDREFS represents the IDREFS attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of IDREFS is the set of finite, non-zero-length sequences of IDREFs. The ·lexical space· of IDREFS is the set of space-separated lists of tokens, of which each token is in the ·lexical space· of IDREF. The ·item type· of IDREFS is IDREF. IDREFS is derived from ·anySimpleType· in two steps: an anonymous list typ

datatypes.name: ENTITY
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] ENTITY represents the ENTITY attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of ENTITY is the set of all strings that ·match· the NCName production in [Namespaces in XML] and have been declared as an unparsed entity in a document type definition. The ·lexical space· of ENTITY is the set of all strings that ·match· the NCName production in [Namespaces in XML]. The ·base type· of ENTITY i

datatypes.name: ENTITIES
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] ENTITIES represents the ENTITIES attribute type from [XML]. The ·value space· of ENTITIES is the set of finite, non-zero-length sequences of ·ENTITY· values that have been declared as unparsed entities in a document type definition. The ·lexical space· of ENTITIES is the set of space-separated lists of tokens, of which each token is in the ·lexical space· of ENTITY. The ·item type· o

datatypes.name: integer
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] integer is ·derived· from decimal by fixing the value of ·fractionDigits· to be 0 and disallowing the trailing decimal point. This results in the standard mathematical concept of the integer numbers. The ·value space· of integer is the infinite set {...,-2,-1,0,1,2,...}. The ·base type· of integer is decimal.

datatypes.name: nonPositiveInteger
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] nonPositiveInteger is ·derived· from integer by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 0. This results in the standard mathematical concept of the non-positive integers. The ·value space· of nonPositiveInteger is the infinite set {...,-2,-1,0}. The ·base type· of nonPositiveInteger is integer.

datatypes.name: negativeInteger
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] negativeInteger is ·derived· from nonPositiveInteger by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be -1. This results in the standard mathematical concept of the negative integers. The ·value space· of negativeInteger is the infinite set {...,-2,-1}. The ·base type· of negativeInteger is nonPositiveInteger.

datatypes.name: long
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] long is ·derived· from integer by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 9223372036854775807 and ·minInclusive· to be -9223372036854775808. The ·base type· of long is integer.

datatypes.name: int
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] int is ·derived· from long by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 2147483647 and ·minInclusive· to be -2147483648. The ·base type· of int is long.

datatypes.name: short
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] short is ·derived· from int by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 32767 and ·minInclusive· to be -32768. The ·base type· of short is int.

datatypes.name: byte
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] byte is ·derived· from short by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 127 and ·minInclusive· to be -128. The ·base type· of byte is short.

datatypes.name: nonNegativeInteger
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] nonNegativeInteger is ·derived· from integer by setting the value of ·minInclusive· to be 0. This results in the standard mathematical concept of the non-negative integers. The ·value space· of nonNegativeInteger is the infinite set {0,1,2,...}. The ·base type· of nonNegativeInteger is integer.

datatypes.name: unsignedLong
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] unsignedLong is ·derived· from nonNegativeInteger by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 18446744073709551615. The ·base type· of unsignedLong is nonNegativeInteger.

datatypes.name: unsignedInt
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] unsignedInt is ·derived· from unsignedLong by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 4294967295. The ·base type· of unsignedInt is unsignedLong.

datatypes.name: unsignedShort
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] unsignedShort is ·derived· from unsignedInt by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 65535. The ·base type· of unsignedShort is unsignedInt.

datatypes.name: unsignedByte
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] unsignedByte is ·derived· from unsignedShort by setting the value of ·maxInclusive· to be 255. The ·base type· of unsignedByte is unsignedShort.

datatypes.name: positiveInteger
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] positiveInteger is ·derived· from nonNegativeInteger by setting the value of ·minInclusive· to be 1. This results in the standard mathematical concept of the positive integer numbers. The ·value space· of positiveInteger is the infinite set {1,2,...}. The ·base type· of positiveInteger is nonNegativeInteger.

datatypes.name: yearMonthDuration
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] yearMonthDuration is a datatype ·derived· from duration by restricting its ·lexical representations· to instances of yearMonthDurationLexicalRep. The ·value space· of yearMonthDuration is therefore that of duration restricted to those whose ·seconds· property is 0. This results in a duration datatype which is totally ordered.

datatypes.name: dayTimeDuration
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] dayTimeDuration is a datatype ·derived· from duration by restricting its ·lexical representations· to instances of dayTimeDurationLexicalRep. The ·value space· of dayTimeDuration is therefore that of duration restricted to those whose ·months· property is 0. This results in a duration datatype which is totally ordered.

datatypes.name: dateTimeStamp
datatypes.prose_excerpt: [Definition:] The dateTimeStamp datatype is ·derived· from dateTime by giving the value required to its explicitTimezone facet. The result is that all values of dateTimeStamp are required to have explicit time zone offsets and the datatype is totally ordered.

datatype_productions.rhs: ( B64 B64 B64 B64 ) /* B64quad represents three octets of binary data. */

datatype_productions.name: B64finalquad
datatype_productions.rhs: ( B64 B64 B64 B64char ) /* B64finalquad represents three octets of binary data without trailing space. */

datatype_productions.name: Padded16
datatype_productions.rhs: B64 B64 B16 ' = ' /* Padded16 represents a two-octet at the end of the data. */

datatype_productions.name: Padded8
datatype_productions.rhs: B64 B04 ' = ' #x20? ' = ' /* Padded8 represents a single octet at the end of the data. */

datatype_productions.name: B16char
datatype_productions.rhs: [AEIMQUYcgkosw048] /* Base64 characters whose bit-string value ends in '00' */

datatype_productions.name: B04char
datatype_productions.rhs: [AQgw] /* Base64 characters whose bit-string value ends in '0000' */

datatype_productions.name: dateTimeStampLexicalRep
datatype_productions.rhs: yearFrag ' - ' monthFrag ' - ' dayFrag ' T ' (( hourFrag ' : ' minuteFrag ' : ' secondFrag ) | endOfDayFrag ) timezoneFrag Constraint: Day-of-month Representations

datatype_productions.name: yearFrag
datatype_productions.rhs: ' - '? (([ 1-9 ] digit digit digit +)) | (' 0 ' digit digit digit ))

datatype_productions.name: IsCategory
datatype_productions.rhs: Letters | Marks | Numbers | Punctuation | Separators | Symbols | Others

core_vocabulary.name: Shape
core_vocabulary.label: Shape
core_vocabulary.comment: A shape is a collection of constraints that may be targeted for certain nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: NodeShape
core_vocabulary.label: Node shape
core_vocabulary.comment: A node shape is a shape that specifies constraint that need to be met with respect to focus nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: PropertyShape
core_vocabulary.label: Property shape
core_vocabulary.comment: A property shape is a shape that specifies constraints on the values of a focus node for a given property or path.

core_vocabulary.name: deactivated
core_vocabulary.label: deactivated
core_vocabulary.comment: If set to true then all nodes conform to this.

core_vocabulary.name: targetClass
core_vocabulary.label: target class
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a shape to a class, indicating that all instances of the class must conform to the shape.

core_vocabulary.name: targetNode
core_vocabulary.label: target node
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a shape to individual nodes, indicating that these nodes must conform to the shape.

core_vocabulary.name: targetObjectsOf
core_vocabulary.label: target objects of
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a shape to a property, indicating that all all objects of triples that have the given property as their predicate must conform to the shape.

core_vocabulary.name: targetSubjectsOf
core_vocabulary.label: target subjects of
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a shape to a property, indicating that all subjects of triples that have the given property as their predicate must conform to the shape.

core_vocabulary.name: message
core_vocabulary.label: message
core_vocabulary.comment: A human-readable message (possibly with placeholders for variables) explaining the cause of the result.

core_vocabulary.name: severity
core_vocabulary.label: severity
core_vocabulary.comment: Defines the severity that validation results produced by a shape must have. Defaults to sh:Violation.

core_vocabulary.name: NodeKind
core_vocabulary.label: Node kind
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of all node kinds, including sh:BlankNode, sh:IRI, sh:Literal or the combinations of these: sh:BlankNodeOrIRI, sh:BlankNodeOrLiteral, sh:IRIOrLiteral.

core_vocabulary.name: BlankNode
core_vocabulary.label: Blank node
core_vocabulary.comment: The node kind of all blank nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: BlankNodeOrIRI
core_vocabulary.label: Blank node or IRI
core_vocabulary.comment: The node kind of all blank nodes or IRIs.

core_vocabulary.name: BlankNodeOrLiteral
core_vocabulary.label: Blank node or literal
core_vocabulary.comment: The node kind of all blank nodes or literals.

core_vocabulary.name: IRI
core_vocabulary.label: IRI
core_vocabulary.comment: The node kind of all IRIs.

core_vocabulary.name: IRIOrLiteral
core_vocabulary.label: IRI or literal
core_vocabulary.comment: The node kind of all IRIs or literals.

core_vocabulary.name: Literal
core_vocabulary.label: Literal
core_vocabulary.comment: The node kind of all literals.

core_vocabulary.name: ValidationReport
core_vocabulary.label: Validation report
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of SHACL validation reports.

core_vocabulary.name: conforms
core_vocabulary.label: conforms
core_vocabulary.comment: True if the validation did not produce any validation results, and false otherwise.

core_vocabulary.name: result
core_vocabulary.label: result
core_vocabulary.comment: The validation results contained in a validation report.

core_vocabulary.name: shapesGraphWellFormed
core_vocabulary.label: shapes graph well-formed
core_vocabulary.comment: If true then the validation engine was certain that the shapes graph has passed all SHACL syntax requirements during the validation process.

core_vocabulary.name: AbstractResult
core_vocabulary.label: Abstract result
core_vocabulary.comment: The base class of validation results, typically not instantiated directly.

core_vocabulary.name: ValidationResult
core_vocabulary.label: Validation result
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of validation results.

core_vocabulary.name: Severity
core_vocabulary.label: Severity
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of validation result severity levels, including violation and warning levels.

core_vocabulary.name: Info
core_vocabulary.label: Info
core_vocabulary.comment: The severity for an informational validation result.

core_vocabulary.name: Violation
core_vocabulary.label: Violation
core_vocabulary.comment: The severity for a violation validation result.

core_vocabulary.name: Warning
core_vocabulary.label: Warning
core_vocabulary.comment: The severity for a warning validation result.

core_vocabulary.name: detail
core_vocabulary.label: detail
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a result with other results that provide more details, for example to describe violations against nested shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: focusNode
core_vocabulary.label: focus node
core_vocabulary.comment: The focus node that was validated when the result was produced.

core_vocabulary.name: resultMessage
core_vocabulary.label: result message
core_vocabulary.comment: Human-readable messages explaining the cause of the result.

core_vocabulary.name: resultPath
core_vocabulary.label: result path
core_vocabulary.comment: The path of a validation result, based on the path of the validated property shape.

core_vocabulary.name: resultSeverity
core_vocabulary.label: result severity
core_vocabulary.comment: The severity of the result, e.g. warning.

core_vocabulary.name: sourceConstraint
core_vocabulary.label: source constraint
core_vocabulary.comment: The constraint that was validated when the result was produced.

core_vocabulary.name: sourceShape
core_vocabulary.label: source shape
core_vocabulary.comment: The shape that is was validated when the result was produced.

core_vocabulary.name: sourceConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: source constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: The constraint component that is the source of the result.

core_vocabulary.name: value
core_vocabulary.label: value
core_vocabulary.comment: An RDF node that has caused the result.

core_vocabulary.name: shapesGraph
core_vocabulary.label: shapes graph
core_vocabulary.comment: Shapes graphs that should be used when validating this data graph.

core_vocabulary.name: suggestedShapesGraph
core_vocabulary.label: suggested shapes graph
core_vocabulary.comment: Suggested shapes graphs for this ontology. The values of this property may be used in the absence of specific sh:shapesGraph statements.

core_vocabulary.name: entailment
core_vocabulary.label: entailment
core_vocabulary.comment: An entailment regime that indicates what kind of inferencing is required by a shapes graph.

core_vocabulary.name: path
core_vocabulary.label: path
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the property path of a property shape.

core_vocabulary.name: inversePath
core_vocabulary.label: inverse path
core_vocabulary.comment: The (single) value of this property represents an inverse path (object to subject).

core_vocabulary.name: alternativePath
core_vocabulary.label: alternative path
core_vocabulary.comment: The (single) value of this property must be a list of path elements, representing the elements of alternative paths.

core_vocabulary.name: zeroOrMorePath
core_vocabulary.label: zero or more path
core_vocabulary.comment: The (single) value of this property represents a path that is matched zero or more times.

core_vocabulary.name: oneOrMorePath
core_vocabulary.label: one or more path
core_vocabulary.comment: The (single) value of this property represents a path that is matched one or more times.

core_vocabulary.name: zeroOrOnePath
core_vocabulary.label: zero or one path
core_vocabulary.comment: The (single) value of this property represents a path that is matched zero or one times.

core_vocabulary.name: Parameterizable
core_vocabulary.label: Parameterizable
core_vocabulary.comment: Superclass of components that can take parameters, especially functions and constraint components.

core_vocabulary.name: parameter
core_vocabulary.label: parameter
core_vocabulary.comment: The parameters of a function or constraint component.

core_vocabulary.name: labelTemplate
core_vocabulary.label: label template
core_vocabulary.comment: Outlines how human-readable labels of instances of the associated Parameterizable shall be produced. The values can contain {?paramName} as placeholders for the actual values of the given parameter.

core_vocabulary.name: Parameter
core_vocabulary.label: Parameter
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of parameter declarations, consisting of a path predicate and (possibly) information about allowed value type, cardinality and other characteristics.

core_vocabulary.name: optional
core_vocabulary.label: optional
core_vocabulary.comment: Indicates whether a parameter is optional.

core_vocabulary.name: ConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of constraint components.

core_vocabulary.name: validator
core_vocabulary.label: validator
core_vocabulary.comment: The validator(s) used to evaluate constraints of either node or property shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: nodeValidator
core_vocabulary.label: shape validator
core_vocabulary.comment: The validator(s) used to evaluate a constraint in the context of a node shape.

core_vocabulary.name: propertyValidator
core_vocabulary.label: property validator
core_vocabulary.comment: The validator(s) used to evaluate a constraint in the context of a property shape.

core_vocabulary.name: Validator
core_vocabulary.label: Validator
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of validators, which provide instructions on how to process a constraint definition. This class serves as base class for the SPARQL-based validators and other possible implementations.

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLAskValidator
core_vocabulary.label: SPARQL ASK validator
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of validators based on SPARQL ASK queries. The queries are evaluated for each value node and are supposed to return true if the given node conforms.

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLSelectValidator
core_vocabulary.label: SPARQL SELECT validator
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of validators based on SPARQL SELECT queries. The queries are evaluated for each focus node and are supposed to produce bindings for all focus nodes that do not conform.

core_vocabulary.name: AndConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: And constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to test whether a value node conforms to all members of a provided list of shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: and
core_vocabulary.label: and
core_vocabulary.comment: RDF list of shapes to validate the value nodes against.

core_vocabulary.name: ClassConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Class constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that each value node is an instance of a given type.

core_vocabulary.name: class
core_vocabulary.label: class
core_vocabulary.comment: The type that all value nodes must have.

core_vocabulary.name: ClosedConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Closed constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to indicate that focus nodes must only have values for those properties that have been explicitly enumerated via sh:property/sh:path.

core_vocabulary.name: closed
core_vocabulary.label: closed
core_vocabulary.comment: If set to true then the shape is closed.

core_vocabulary.name: ignoredProperties
core_vocabulary.label: ignored properties
core_vocabulary.comment: An optional RDF list of properties that are also permitted in addition to those explicitly enumerated via sh:property/sh:path.

core_vocabulary.name: DatatypeConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Datatype constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the datatype of all value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: datatype
core_vocabulary.label: datatype
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies an RDF datatype that all value nodes must have.

core_vocabulary.name: DisjointConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Disjoint constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that the set of value nodes is disjoint with the the set of nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

core_vocabulary.name: disjoint
core_vocabulary.label: disjoint
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a property where the set of values must be disjoint with the value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: EqualsConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Equals constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that the set of value nodes is equal to the set of nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

core_vocabulary.name: equals
core_vocabulary.label: equals
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a property that must have the same values as the value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: HasValueConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Has-value constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that one of the value nodes is a given RDF node.

core_vocabulary.name: hasValue
core_vocabulary.label: has value
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a value that must be among the value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: InConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: In constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to exclusively enumerate the permitted value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: in
core_vocabulary.label: in
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a list of allowed values so that each value node must be among the members of the given list.

core_vocabulary.name: LanguageInConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Language-in constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to enumerate language tags that all value nodes must have.

core_vocabulary.name: languageIn
core_vocabulary.label: language in
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a list of language tags that all value nodes must have.

core_vocabulary.name: LessThanConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Less-than constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that each value node is smaller than all the nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

core_vocabulary.name: lessThan
core_vocabulary.label: less than
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a property that must have smaller values than the value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: LessThanOrEqualsConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: less-than-or-equals constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that every value node is smaller than all the nodes that have the focus node as subject and the value of a given property as predicate.

core_vocabulary.name: lessThanOrEquals
core_vocabulary.label: less than or equals
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a property that must have smaller or equal values than the value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: MaxCountConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Max-count constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the maximum number of value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: maxCount
core_vocabulary.label: max count
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the maximum number of values in the set of value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: MaxExclusiveConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Max-exclusive constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a maximum exclusive value.

core_vocabulary.name: maxExclusive
core_vocabulary.label: max exclusive
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the maximum exclusive value of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: MaxInclusiveConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Max-inclusive constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a maximum inclusive value.

core_vocabulary.name: maxInclusive
core_vocabulary.label: max inclusive
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the maximum inclusive value of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: MaxLengthConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Max-length constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the maximum string length of value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: maxLength
core_vocabulary.label: max length
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the maximum string length of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: MinCountConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Min-count constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the minimum number of value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: minCount
core_vocabulary.label: min count
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the minimum number of values in the set of value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: MinExclusiveConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Min-exclusive constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a minimum exclusive value.

core_vocabulary.name: minExclusive
core_vocabulary.label: min exclusive
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the minimum exclusive value of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: MinInclusiveConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Min-inclusive constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the range of value nodes with a minimum inclusive value.

core_vocabulary.name: minInclusive
core_vocabulary.label: min inclusive
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the minimum inclusive value of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: MinLengthConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Min-length constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the minimum string length of value nodes.

core_vocabulary.name: minLength
core_vocabulary.label: min length
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the minimum string length of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: NodeConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Node constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that all value nodes conform to the given node shape.

core_vocabulary.name: node
core_vocabulary.label: node
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the node shape that all value nodes must conform to.

core_vocabulary.name: NodeKindConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Node-kind constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the RDF node kind of each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: nodeKind
core_vocabulary.label: node kind
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies the node kind (e.g. IRI or literal) each value node.

core_vocabulary.name: NotConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Not constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that value nodes do not conform to a given shape.

core_vocabulary.name: not
core_vocabulary.label: not
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a shape that the value nodes must not conform to.

core_vocabulary.name: OrConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Or constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the value nodes so that they conform to at least one out of several provided shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: or
core_vocabulary.label: or
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a list of shapes so that the value nodes must conform to at least one of the shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: PatternConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Pattern constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that every value node matches a given regular expression.

core_vocabulary.name: flags
core_vocabulary.label: flags
core_vocabulary.comment: An optional flag to be used with regular expression pattern matching.

core_vocabulary.name: pattern
core_vocabulary.label: pattern
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a regular expression pattern that the string representations of the value nodes must match.

core_vocabulary.name: PropertyConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Property constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that all value nodes conform to the given property shape.

core_vocabulary.name: property
core_vocabulary.label: property
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a shape to its property shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: QualifiedMaxCountConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Qualified-max-count constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that a specified maximum number of value nodes conforms to a given shape.

core_vocabulary.name: QualifiedMinCountConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Qualified-min-count constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to verify that a specified minimum number of value nodes conforms to a given shape.

core_vocabulary.name: qualifiedMaxCount
core_vocabulary.label: qualified max count
core_vocabulary.comment: The maximum number of value nodes that can conform to the shape.

core_vocabulary.name: qualifiedMinCount
core_vocabulary.label: qualified min count
core_vocabulary.comment: The minimum number of value nodes that must conform to the shape.

core_vocabulary.name: qualifiedValueShape
core_vocabulary.label: qualified value shape
core_vocabulary.comment: The shape that a specified number of values must conform to.

core_vocabulary.name: qualifiedValueShapesDisjoint
core_vocabulary.label: qualified value shapes disjoint
core_vocabulary.comment: Can be used to mark the qualified value shape to be disjoint with its sibling shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: UniqueLangConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Unique-languages constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to specify that no pair of value nodes may use the same language tag.

core_vocabulary.name: uniqueLang
core_vocabulary.label: unique languages
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies whether all node values must have a unique (or no) language tag.

core_vocabulary.name: XoneConstraintComponent
core_vocabulary.label: Exactly one constraint component
core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to restrict the value nodes so that they conform to exactly one out of several provided shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: xone
core_vocabulary.label: exactly one
core_vocabulary.comment: Specifies a list of shapes so that the value nodes must conform to exactly one of the shapes.

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLExecutable

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLAskExecutable

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLConstructExecutable

core_vocabulary.name: construct

core_vocabulary.comment: The SPARQL CONSTRUCT query to execute.

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLSelectExecutable
core_vocabulary.label: SPARQL SELECT executable
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of SPARQL executables based on a SELECT query.

core_vocabulary.name: select
core_vocabulary.label: select
core_vocabulary.comment: The SPARQL SELECT query to execute.

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLUpdateExecutable
core_vocabulary.label: SPARQL UPDATE executable
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of SPARQL executables based on a SPARQL UPDATE.

core_vocabulary.name: update
core_vocabulary.label: update
core_vocabulary.comment: The SPARQL UPDATE to execute.

core_vocabulary.name: prefixes
core_vocabulary.label: prefixes
core_vocabulary.comment: The prefixes that shall be applied before parsing the associated SPARQL query.

core_vocabulary.name: PrefixDeclaration
core_vocabulary.label: Prefix declaration
core_vocabulary.comment: The class of prefix declarations, consisting of pairs of a prefix with a namespace.

core_vocabulary.name: declare
core_vocabulary.label: declare
core_vocabulary.comment: Links a resource with its namespace prefix declarations.

core_vocabulary.name: prefix
core_vocabulary.label: prefix
core_vocabulary.comment: The prefix of a prefix declaration.

core_vocabulary.name: namespace
core_vocabulary.label: namespace
core_vocabulary.comment: The namespace associated with a prefix in a prefix declaration.

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLConstraintComponent

core_vocabulary.comment: A constraint component that can be used to define constraints based on SPARQL queries.

core_vocabulary.name: sparql

core_vocabulary.name: SPARQLConstraint
